content.summary: Nym Health (nym.health) is a New York City-based health IT company founded in 2018 by Amihai Neiderman and Adam Rimon (alumni of Israel's Unit 8200). It is the market leader in autonomous medical coding, using a proprietary Clinical Language Understanding (CLU) engine and explainable AI to automatically transform physician chart notes into ICD-10-CM and CPT billing codes — with zero human intervention, >95% accuracy, and a 12-hour TAT. The platform integrates via FHIR with major EHRs (Epic, Cerner Oracle Health, Meditech) and serves 400+ U.S. healthcare facilities, processing 6M+ charts annually. Nym has raised ~$63.5M+ across known rounds, most recently a $47M growth investment led by PSG (October 2024), with co-investors GV, Addition, Samsung Next, Bessemer, Dynamic Loop, and Tiger Global. As of July 2026, Nym has approximately 287 LinkedIn-listed employees with offices in New York (HQ) and Tel Aviv (R&D). CEO Lori M. Jones (joined ~May 2025) leads a recently rebuilt C-suite including CCO Julien Dubuis, CFO Yuval Yezersky, and SVP R&D Yaniv Shachar. Key buying signals include the 2025 CEO transition, active senior hiring (including a Chief Customer Officer search), continued multi-specialty product expansion, and recognition as an 'Outstanding' HealthTech company by TIME in 2025.
content.industry: Health Information Technology / AI-Driven Revenue Cycle Management (Autonomous Medical Coding)
tech_stack: Proprietary Clinical Language Understanding (CLU) / NLP engine (core AI/ML for autonomous medical coding)
FHIR-based EHR integration (standards-based HL7 FHIR API)
HubSpot / go.nym.health (gated content and marketing automation)
Cloud infrastructure (AI-first; R&D in Tel Aviv)
content.headquarters: 45 Bond St, New York, NY 10012, USA (with R&D office in Tel Aviv, Israel)
content.funding_stage: Growth-stage (post-Series A; $47M growth round led by PSG, October 2024)
content.total_funding: ~$63.5M+ publicly disclosed (undisclosed seed + $16.5M Series A in 2020 + $47M growth round in 2024)
buying_signals: New CEO hire (Lori M. Jones, ~May 2025): leadership change creating new strategic and budget priorities
$47M PSG-led growth investment (October 2024): actively deploying capital for product expansion and sales/marketing scale-up
Actively hiring: open roles for Chief Customer Officer, Customer Success Executive (Health Systems), and multiple senior positions posted in 2025–2026
Multi-specialty expansion: broadening from emergency medicine to urgent care, radiology, and additional specialties — new buyer segments being targeted
Named to TIME's World's Top HealthTech Companies of 2025 ('Outstanding' rank, September 2025): elevated brand profile driving inbound and partnership interest
Rapid customer base growth: 400+ healthcare facilities and 6M+ charts processed annually (as of 2024), up from 40 providers in 2020
Multiple senior leadership additions in 2025–2026 (CFO, SVP R&D, VP Product, VP Implementation, VP Compliance): building enterprise-grade execution team
Active thought leadership and HFMA partnership roundtables in 2025–2026: top-of-funnel engagement signaling commercial expansion push
content.headcount_estimate: ~287 employees (LinkedIn, July 2026); LinkedIn company size band: 51–200
